nodes.name: design-database
nodes.description: Use when designing or revising a database schema — produce an entity model in dbdiagram (DBML) form with keys, relationships, and indexes, then write DATABASE_DESIGN.md

nodes.name: design-frontend
nodes.description: Use when generating UI from a style direction, a prompt, or a reference URL — produce a concrete component/layout design before implementing it

nodes.name: design-system
nodes.description: Use when establishing or reviewing design tokens and a component vocabulary — colors, type scale, spacing, radii, and a mock UI to prove them

nodes.name: dev-audit
nodes.description: Use to pressure-test a proposed idea, design, or plan before committing to it — surface feasibility, risks, unknowns, and a go / no-go recommendation

nodes.name: dev-brainstorm
nodes.description: Use before creative work — features, components, behavior changes. Explores intent and design before implementation.

nodes.name: dev-commit
nodes.description: Use when the user explicitly asks to commit — conventional message plus Plan trailer during plan work

nodes.name: dev-debug
nodes.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

nodes.name: dev-execute
nodes.description: Use when implementing a written plan in docs/plans/ — task-by-task with verification checkpoints

nodes.name: dev-parallel
nodes.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks with no shared state or sequential dependency — dispatch one focused subagent per problem domain to work concurrently

nodes.name: dev-plan
nodes.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

nodes.name: dev-refactor
nodes.description: Use for dedicated dedupe/cleanup sprints across a repo or domain area — inventory clones, consolidate, reshape modules, delete dead code. Not for PR-time diff passes (use review-simplify).

nodes.name: dev-research
nodes.description: Use when the user wants thorough, cited research from multiple web sources before deciding or building — "research", "deep dive", "investigate", "what's the current state of"

nodes.name: dev-tdd
nodes.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

nodes.name: dev-verify
nodes.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence

nodes.name: doc-init
nodes.description: Scaffold the minimum project doc set for Luna Agent Kit — idempotent, never overwrites existing files. Wires both Claude Code and Cursor.

nodes.name: doc-simplify
nodes.description: Use when a doc grew too long, fragmented, or repetitive — right-size it by trimming, merging, splitting, and deduping without losing facts

nodes.name: doc-update-agent
nodes.description: Use when plan/backlog status changed or the user corrected an approach — sync PLANS.md/TODO.md and append lessons to the rules

nodes.name: doc-update-project
nodes.description: Use after a code change alters architecture, structure, schema, or API — sync the human/architecture docs so they match the code

nodes.name: gitnexus-group-setup
nodes.description: Use when a project has git submodules with independent GitNexus indexes — wire them into a group so group_query and group_contracts search across all repos in one call

nodes.name: hook-author
nodes.description: Use when creating or editing a Claude Code / Cursor hook — wire hooks.json events, matchers, and scripts with the kit's fail-open and cross-tool conventions

nodes.name: kwb-api
nodes.description: Use when designing or reviewing REST endpoints — resource naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error shape, versioning, and rate limiting

nodes.name: kwb-deploy
nodes.description: Use when setting up CI/CD, planning a deployment strategy, adding health checks, validating env config, or preparing a production release

nodes.name: kwb-docker
nodes.description: Use when setting up Docker Compose for local dev, writing/reviewing Dockerfiles, or debugging container networking, volumes, or security

nodes.name: kwb-frontend
nodes.description: Use when building or reviewing React/Next.js UI — component composition, hooks, state, performance, forms, error boundaries, and accessibility

nodes.name: kwb-nextjs
nodes.description: Use when developing, debugging, or reviewing a Next.js 16+ / Turbopack app — dev speed, bundling, and the proxy.ts middleware naming change

nodes.name: kwb-postgres
nodes.description: Use when writing SQL or migrations, designing a Postgres schema, choosing indexes, implementing Row Level Security, or diagnosing slow queries

nodes.name: kwb-python
nodes.description: Use when writing or reviewing Python — idiomatic patterns, type hints, error handling, context managers, dataclasses, concurrency, and anti-patterns

nodes.name: kwb-python-testing
nodes.description: Use when writing or reviewing Python tests — pytest fixtures, parametrization, mocking, async tests, markers, coverage, and TDD discipline

nodes.name: kwb-typescript
nodes.description: Use when writing or reviewing TypeScript/JavaScript for baseline quality — naming, immutability, type safety, error handling, and code smells

nodes.name: review-code
nodes.description: Use when completing tasks, major features, or before merge — standalone correctness review

nodes.name: review-performance
nodes.description: Use when reviewing hot paths, loops over data, DB access, or large allocations — catch N+1 queries, needless work, and avoidable allocations

nodes.name: review-security
nodes.description: Use when changing auth, handling user input, touching secrets/credentials, adding API endpoints, or implementing payment/sensitive features — security review against OWASP basics

nodes.name: review-simplify
nodes.description: Use after code changes to reduce duplication, dead code, and unnecessary complexity within the diff — preserve behavior. For repo-wide cleanup sprints use dev-refactor instead.

nodes.name: skill-audit
nodes.description: Use to review the installed skill set for drift, staleness, oversize, broken references, or redundancy — keeps the kit from rotting into bloat

nodes.name: skill-author
nodes.description: Use when creating or editing a skill — author and verify SKILL.md with a test-first, behavior-shaping discipline before deploying

nodes.name: skill-compare
nodes.description: Use when deciding whether to adopt, update, or drop a skill versus the reference forks — diff our version against superpowers/ECC/cpo and recommend

nodes.name: vibe-rules
nodes.description: Use when you need generic engineering rules (vibe coding loop, failure modes, implementation, fixing, testing, GitNexus) — project-agnostic; invoke instead of improvising process or duplicating rules

nodes.name: workflow-guide
nodes.description: Use when starting any conversation — how to find skills, read WORKFLOW.md, and when to use Luna vs native workflows

nodes.name: workflow-update
nodes.description: Use when changing project workflow phases, gates, suggested_skills, variants, or the inline Mermaid diagram in WORKFLOW.md

nodes.name: dev-brainstorm
nodes.description: Use to run a Socratic design session as a subagent — explore a problem space through questions, surface assumptions and alternatives, and return a design direction. The agent form of the dev-brainstor

nodes.name: document-agent
nodes.description: Use to apply the doc-update-agent rubric to PLANS/TODO + lessons in an isolated context — sync plan/backlog status and append corrections to the rules. One of two doc agents (agent-state class).

nodes.name: document-project
nodes.description: Use to apply the doc-update-project / doc-simplify rubric to named paths in an isolated context — sync architecture/structure/schema/API docs after a code change. One of two doc agents (project class)

nodes.name: execute
nodes.description: Use to implement ONE plan task in an isolated context — given a self-contained task spec, write the code and tests, verify, and return a summary. No bundled review (review is a separate, user-invoked

nodes.name: refactor-cleaner
nodes.description: Dead code cleanup and deduplication specialist for multi-session refactor sprints. Use for repo-wide or domain-area cleanup — not for PR-time diff passes (use review-simplify via review-internal).

nodes.name: review-external
nodes.description: Use to collect human/user-facing UI/UX feedback on a running app from distinct user personas, then consolidate into a Blocker/Friction/Nice-to-have acceptance matrix. User-invoked; owns the acceptance

nodes.name: review-internal
nodes.description: Use at PR time or before merge to run a consolidated internal review (correctness, security, performance, simplification) and return one merged report. User-invoked; runs in isolated context so there

nodes.name: test
nodes.description: Use to run the tests for a scoped change in an isolated context and return evidence — which tests ran, pass/fail counts, and the failure output. Does not fix code.
